sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Fund Tx3 by TxPipe as an open API layer for Cardano dApp protocols, including standardized protocol interfaces, documentation, multi-language SDKs, live JSON-RPC endpoints, agent-facing documentation, protocol skills packaging, and an MCP server.
Recipient: TxPipe via Intersect treasury management smart contracts

Deliverables: Open API Layer for Cardano Protocols, 12 additional protocol onboardings over 12 months, described as 3 per quarter, Agent-first documentation, Protocol skills packaging operations, MCP server for agents to discover and call published protocols, Shared infrastructure for protocols on the Tx3 registry

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 1,308,000 ADA plus a 327,000 ADA contingency reserve over 12 months, with a total withdrawal of 1,684,050 ADA including a 49,050 ADA Intersect administration fee.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Tx3 is intended to provide a single standardized interface layer through which developers or AI agents can discover, integrate, and compose Cardano on-chain protocols.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says Cardano currently lacks a common interface mechanism across protocols, forcing integrators to relearn each protocol and rebuild understanding independently.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says Tx3 describes each protocol once with a verified interface definition that produces documentation, auto-generated SDKs in multiple languages, and live JSON-RPC endpoints.

Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): Through Catalyst Fund 14, TxPipe has already put the developer layer into production with 5 protocols live on the Tx3 Protocol Registry.
Claim (adoption, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal says the funded work will onboard 12 additional protocols, 3 per quarter, with developer and agent coverage.
missing: Independent evidence for: Tx3 is intended to provide a single standardized interface layer through which developers or AI agents can discover, integrate, and compose Cardano on-chain protocols.

Independent evidence for: The proposal says Cardano currently lacks a common interface mechanism across protocols, forcing integrators to relearn each protocol and rebuild understanding independently.

Independent evidence for: The proposal says Tx3 describes each protocol once with a verified interface definition that produces documentation, auto-generated SDKs in multiple languages, and live JSON-RPC endpoints.

Independent evidence for: The proposal says audit and oversight costs are included in overhead, with independent oversight by Intersect and technically capable third parties, reporting obligations, and milestone-based disbursement controls.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 1,308,000 ADA plus a 327,000 ADA contingency reserve over 12 months, with a total withdrawal of 1,684,050 ADA including a 49,050 ADA Intersect administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Tx3 is intended to provide a single standardized interface layer through which developers or AI agents can discover, integrate, and compose Cardano on-chain protocols." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
